contract.description: Focus on mistakes, patterns, or non-obvious insights.

selection.selected_title: Are AI Agents Your Next Security Nightmare? (The 4 Security Mistakes I Keep Seeing in Real Projects)
selection.selected_reason: Directly aligned with AI systems / agents, and it lets you speak from real implementation experience: insecure tool configs, over-trusted agents, missing audit trails, and ‘shadow agents’ spun up by teams without governance. It’s practical, slightly contrarian (most people only think about model/data risk, not agent orchestration risk), and perfect for a carousel on “what people get wrong” and non-obvious failure modes.
backup_titles: Gen AI Crash Courses Won’t Make You Dangerous: What They Never Teach You About Shipping Real LLM Systems
Tactical Wins, Strategic Losses: How Teams ‘Succeed’ with LLM POCs and Still Fail at AI Transformation
caution_notes: Keep it specific to agent security patterns you’ve actually seen or can plausibly reason about (e.g., tool mis-scoping, prompt injection through external tools, unbounded actions), not abstract “AI is scary” talk.
Avoid generic security checklists; anchor each slide in one concrete mistake, one pattern, and one tradeoff (security vs speed, flexibility vs control).
If referencing the original KDnuggets piece, don’t summarize it—disagree, extend, or narrow it to a sharper, opinionated angle relevant to builders shipping agents in production.
